title: astelia graminea
intro: Astelia graminea, also known as the golden Astelia, is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand. It is a perennial plant with long, narrow leaves and a spike of golden flowers that grow in the summer. The medicinal properties of Astelia graminea include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing due to its high levels of saponins and polyphenols. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving skin conditions, and aiding in the treatment of wounds, with herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves being used to harness its medicinal properties.
